metadata.region_focus: Zahir Pir / Rahim Yar Khan cotton belt

references.authors: Tomar S, Sharma S, Malik K
references.title: Life Parameters of Whitefly (Bemisia tabaci, Genn.) on Different Host Plants
references.journal: Indian Journal of Science and Research
values_informed: egg.required_gdd — incubation 2.47 days on cotton (shortest of all hosts)
nymph.required_gdd — total nymphal period 8.14 days on cotton
lifecycle total on cotton 20.93 days (complete life cycle, shortest of all hosts tested)
reproduction_model.reproduction_rate — eggs per female on cotton 174.3 (highest of all hosts)
adult longevity on cotton: female 7.55 days, male 6.94 days
cotton confirmed most suitable host — highest growth index 9.74 and shortest developmental period 12.52 days egg-to-adult
11-15 generations per year; adult longevity 10-15 days in summer, 30-60 days in winter

references.title: Effect of temperature and relative humidity on the population dynamics of brinjal and tomato infesting whitefly, Bemisia tabaci
references.journal: Jahangirnagar University Journal of Biological Sciences
values_informed: thermal_properties.optimal_temperature_range — strong positive correlation r=0.972 between B. tabaci population and average temperature
upper_temperature_threshold — high temperature causes desiccation of immature stages, reducing population
humidity_preferences — extreme RH both high and low unfavorable for immature survival (Gerling et al. 1986 cited)
humidity_stress_threshold — dry conditions more favorable than high precipitation; excessive rain substantially reduces population
suitability_weights.rainfall 0.2 — rainfall negatively associated with population build-up

references.authors: Sani I, Ismail SI, Abdullah S, Jalinas J, Jamian S, Saad N
references.title: A Review of the Biology and Control of Whitefly, Bemisia tabaci (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae), with Special Reference to Biological Control Using Entomopathogenic Fungi
values_informed: thermal_properties.optimal_temperature_range — 28°C most favorable temperature for egg production
egg.required_gdd — egg incubation 5-9 days depending on host, temperature, humidity
lifecycle egg-to-adult 16-31 days across conditions
nymph.vulnerability.biological_vulnerability 0.85 — 2nd instar most susceptible stage to EPF biological control

intervention_sensitivity.biological_control_sensitivity elevated to high — EPF (Beauveria bassiana, Metarhizium anisopliae, Isaria fumosorosea, Lecanicillium lecanii, Aschersonia spp.) all confirmed effective; >50-99% mortality documented
intervention_sensitivity.treatment_resistance_risk elevated to high — resistance to neonicotinoids documented; excessive chemical use driving resistance in Pakistan populations
damage_profile adult economic_damage_weight 0.9 — adults transmit 200+ plant viruses including Cotton Leaf Curl Burewala/Multan/Kokhran viruses causing millions USD losses annually
adult mobility high — adults emerge through inverted T-slit, fly and oviposit across canopy; feeding on underside of leaves
